theme_notes: Today's touchstones: US-Iran peace talks collapse as Trump cancels Witkoff/Kushner's Pakistan trip (day 56 of the Middle East conflict), Meta laying off 8,000 employees (10% of workforce) to fund $135B in AI spending, the EU's $106B Ukraine loan clearing after months of deadlock, the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended another 3 weeks, and Pope Leo opposing the death penalty hours after Trump's DOJ approved federal firing squads.
settled: The EU found $106B for Ukraine. You two found zero. Inspiring.
Zuck laid off 8,000 people for efficiency. You two remain uncorrected.
The ceasefire got a 3-week extension. This balance gets nothing.
youOwe: Less than Witkoff's flight to Islamabad that Trump cancelled last minute.
Smaller than a Meta severance check. Somehow still a personality trait.
Pope Leo calls the death penalty inadmissible. Your debt: merely embarrassing.
Not enough for a flight to Islamabad. Enough for one sustained cold shoulder.
The EU mobilized $106B in five days. You've mobilized excuses for weeks.
Israel and Lebanon extended a ceasefire. You can't extend a Venmo request.
A Meta engineer just got laid off to fund AI. You're the inefficiency it replaced.
The EU found $106B for Ukraine in a week. You haven't found your card.
Iran is closer to a US peace deal than you are to clearing this tab.
Witkoff flew to the Middle East and back for nothing. You can't open a banking app.
Zuck is spending $135B on AI because humans disappoint him. Exhibit A.
Pope Leo forgives sins of the soul. Financial sins: different department entirely.
Day 56 of the Iran standoff. Unknown day of this tab. One is in active talks.
Meta fired 8,000 people to fund the future. You're still funding the past.
The ceasefire's been renewed three times. This balance hasn't been touched once.
The EU lent Ukraine $106B with fewer strings attached. Think about that.
Zuckerberg is building AGI. You're building a case for why this 'isn't clear.'
Pope Leo called firing squads inadmissible. Your creditor is reconsidering their stance.
theyOwe: Iran won't meet US envoys directly. They handle your Venmos the same way.
Meta laid off 8,000 for inefficiency. Zuck would have thoughts about this.
Smaller than the cost of Kushner's cancelled Pakistan flight. Still owed.
Less than a minibar tab at the Islamabad hotel nobody used. Still counts.
The EU moved $106B for Ukraine this week. Nobody moved anything for you.
About what Iran spent refusing to meet US diplomats. You know that feeling.
Zuck just cleared 8,000 headcount. One more would be statistically painless.
Iran agreed to stop the missiles. They won't stop sitting on this balance.
Israel and Lebanon shook hands at the White House. A Venmo note would also work.
Iran peace talks are stuck on semantics. So is your repayment discussion.
Zuck cut 8,000 humans to save money. You're considering a smaller-scale version.
The EU guaranteed $106B in writing. You don't even have a screenshot.
Russia launched 600 drones this week. You sent one polite text. Results differ.
Meta is investing $135B in AI. AI still cannot fix a freeloading partner.
Pope Leo calls firing squads inadmissible. You're keeping all options open.
Not quite the EU's $106B Ukraine loan. Equally unclear if it'll ever be repaid.
US-Iran peace talks have more forward momentum than your repayment plan.
Zuckerberg replaced 8,000 people with AI. You'd settle for the one who owes you.
